MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lee E. Sheldon of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant / moving party Yaeger and Kirk (a dissolved corporation)
Lisa Brokaw of LEVIN SIMES LLP (415-426-3000), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Judith Rhodes

RULING:
DEFENDANT YAEGER AND KIRK (A DISSOLVED CORPORATION)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Yaeger and Kirk's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden via the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, defendant sustained its initial burden on the issue of whether plaintiff was exposed to any dust from a product supplied by/purchased from Yaeger and Kirk. Defendant concedes that plaintiff has evidence that her father and/or brother purchased Kaiser Gypsum and/or Georgia Pacific joint compound from defendant. Defendant does not argue that plaintiff does not possess and cannot obtain evidence that such joint compound(s) contained asbestos and, even if defendant had made that an issue, plaintiff's responses to defendant's special interrogatories would preclude defendant from sustaining its burden on that topic. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of Daniel Tinney and the declaration of John Templin create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiff's objections to the declaration of Donald Yaeger are overruled, however Mr. Yaeger's declaration is not sufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled and its objections to the interrogatory responses of Kaiser Gypsum and Georgia Pacific are sustained. Defendant's objections to John Templin's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 15 (the 5th and 6th bullet points only), 35, 37, 38 and 39 and to Exhibits F and G attached thereto and are overruled as to all other objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE W.W. HENRY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether she was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's Exhibits G, H, J, and N are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's Exhibits I and K are sustained as inadmissible hearsay. Defendant's objections to the declaration of John Templin are sustained as to paragraphs 31, 47-59 and Exhibits H, K and L attached thereto and overruled as to all other objections. RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTIONS 36(A), (D), & (E)

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a), (d) and (e). Trial date is September 26, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. 120th day is October 6, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 15, 2016. Plaintiff has stipulated that summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on 35 days' notice. Oppositions and replies due pursuant to C.C.P. 437c. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 12, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 26, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by September 19, 2016 at 12:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on September 21, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 9, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 23, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 8, 2016. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than June 22, 2016 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than June 22, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry)
